title: Doc/test/tutorial templates + canonical contribution model
context: Every contribution this project makes needs to follow a consistent structure so that it is reproducible, correctly attributed, legally compliant, and acceptable to upstream maintainers. Without templates and a canonical contribution model, each contributor risks missing mandatory elements: data-provenance header, 'not medical advice' disclaimer, citation format, environment-pin approach, and DCO sign-off guidance.
objective: Publish doc/test/tutorial templates and a canonical contribution model that every contributor reuses, covering the mandatory elements: data-provenance and license header, 'research/education use — not medical advice' disclaimer, citation format for biological/clinical assertions, pinned-environment block, and DCO sign-off guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: A docstring template for Python and R is published covering required fields: description, parameters, returns, example, and a data-provenance note where applicable.

A tutorial template is published with mandatory sections: data-provenance and license header, 'research/education use — not medical advice' disclaimer, pinned environment block, citations section, and a reproduction-instructions block.
A test template is published covering fixture/data provenance, assertion style, and the CI run reference.

A canonical contribution model document is published defining fields for each contribution (id, targetTool, contributionType, gap, exampleData, claims, disclaimerIncluded, reproEnv, review, acceptance, coverage) as specified in PLAN.md.
Templates are committed and referenced from the project contributing guide so contributors can immediately use them for the pilot (pilot-007) and subsequent contributions.
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach & architecture (Canonical contribution model section)
output: Committed doc/test/tutorial templates and a canonical contribution model document, ready for immediate reuse by pilot-007 and all subsequent contributions.
